title: Papers I found exciting
intro: The frontier moves every month. These are the June 2026 papers that made me stop scrolling — picked from the DeepTech Radar feed for what they make newly possible and the white space they open. (Sample issue — the take-lines and this intro get rewritten in my own voice before publishing.)

papers.title: Atmospheric methane-to-CO2 oxidation catalysts active at ambient temperature and 2 ppm
papers.field: Methane removal and negative emissions technology
papers.why: The study presents a heterogeneous catalyst that selectively oxidizes methane at atmospheric concentrations (~2 ppm) to CO2 at near-ambient temperature with minimal energy input, increasing the effective radiative forcing reduction per unit

papers.title: Catalyst-free electroreduction of nitrate wastewater to ammonia for circular fertilizer production
papers.institution: IIT Bombay Department of Chemical Engineering
papers.field: Green ammonia, wastewater valorization, and circular nitrogen economy
papers.why: The paper reports an electrochemical reactor that converts industrial nitrate-containing wastewater streams directly to ammonia with high faradaic efficiency using earth-abundant electrodes and no precious metal catalysts.

papers.title: Exciton-driven Floquet engineering of quantum materials at low drive power
papers.institution: Okinawa Institute of Science and Technology (OIST) & Stanford University
papers.why: The team showed that instead of using extremely intense laser fields to induce Floquet phases, one can use **excitons**â€”bound electronâ€“hole pairsâ€”inside semiconductors to drive equivalent nonequilibrium quantum states with orders-of-m

papers.title: Spinâ€‘flip molybdenum complexes enable >100% exciton harvesting for nextâ€‘generation solar cells
papers.institution: Kyushu University Center for Organic Photonics and Electronics Research (OPERA)
papers.why: Researchers engineered a molybdenumâ€‘based **spinâ€‘flip emitter** that captures triplet excitons generated by singlet fission, achieving quantum yields around 130%, i.e., more than one energy carrier per absorbed photon in solution.[1] Th

papers.title: Swaraj: Multi-Agent LLM Orchestration for Telecom Network Operations
papers.institution: IIT Madras Robert Bosch Centre for Data Science and AI (RBCDSAI)
papers.field: Network management agents and telco AI infrastructure
papers.why: Demonstrates a system of cooperating LLM agents that ingest OSS/BSS logs, topology graphs, and traffic KPIs to autonomously propose, simulate, and enact network configuration changes under operator-defined safety constraints.

papers.title: De novo designed virus-like particles for targeted mRNA delivery to hematopoietic stem cells
papers.institution: Institute for Protein Design, University of Washington
papers.why: Using deep-learning-guided protein design, the study reports self-assembling virus-like particles that selectively bind hematopoietic stem cells and package mRNA cargos without using viral components.

papers.title: Programmable optogenetic control of CAR-T cell exhaustion via synthetic transcriptional regulators
papers.why: The researchers engineered CAR-T cells with light-responsive synthetic transcription factors that dynamically modulate exhaustion pathways such as TOX and NR4A in response to non-invasive light cues.

papers.title: High-efficiency electrochemical cement decarbonization via integrated calcium looping and CO2 mineralization
papers.institution: MIT Department of Materials Science and Engineering
papers.why: The work demonstrates an electrochemical cement production route that replaces limestone calcination with an electrically driven calcium-looping process, producing a pure CO2 stream for easy capture while dramatically reducing process emiss
